mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.1 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observed cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects sustained capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases produce negative 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes when filtered by NBER recession dating.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands aggregate corporate revenue pools, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 valuations that materializes as positive returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes correlate with negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal yields negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of aligned observations, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly housing starts above 1.2 million units signal strong residential investment demand that spills over into broader equity markets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST values above 1.2 million units precede negative 20-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive significance when tested outside periods of rising mortgage applications.
